_protocol.what_this_file_is: signal.json is the mutable, provisional companion to logs.json. Where logs.json is concrete, authoritative, and binding — signal.json is fluid, debatable, and in-progress. It holds tasks that may or may not be done, issues that may or may not be fixed, and proposals that were offered but not necessarily chosen. The laws and rules in logs.json still govern all work. Nothing in this file overrides them.
_protocol.the_contract: This file is NOT a rulebook. It is a working surface. Its contents are provisional — tasks can be cancelled, issues can be closed, proposals can be discarded. An LLM must not treat entries here as confirmed requirements unless status is 'agreed' or 'in_progress'.

when_to_update.tasks: Add a task when: (1) trap explicitly says so mid-session ('log that', 'add to signal'), OR (2) trap makes a single request containing multiple feature asks — log each as a separate task. LLM may auto-close tasks as 'no_longer_relevant' or 'irrelevant' if context clearly warrants it — mark closed_by: 'llm'. Trap-closed tasks use closed_by: 'trap'. These are styled differently in the viewer.
when_to_update.issues: Add an issue when: (1) trap explicitly asks, OR (2) the LLM encounters a Critical-severity problem during any session — proactively flag these even without being asked. Warning and Note issues are only logged when explicitly discussed. Always include scope and reproduction_note (a one-sentence description of how to reproduce or observe the problem — for resolved issues, describe what the state was before the fix).
when_to_update.proposals: Add a proposal entry whenever the LLM offers multiple options and trap selects one — log ALL options including the chosen one. Required fields: context (what was asked, reworded to focus on the request only — no explicit commands or bad phrasing), summary (the proposal itself), why_guess (1-sentence LLM guess on why chosen or not chosen), session_date (YYYY-MM-DD of the session it was offered), chosen (true/false/null for pending). Total characters of context + summary + why_guess must not exceed 256 (spaces excluded). Tags: max 3, short, not counted toward 256.

_protocol.rule_promotion: If the LLM is ≥90% confident that something in this file should be promoted to a rule in logs.json (rules.absolute or rules.strong_preferences), it MUST surface this as an explicit question to trap — it may NOT edit logs.json rules unilaterally. State clearly: what the rule would be, why confidence is high, and ask for confirmation.

tasks.scratch: Raw idea, not yet evaluated — may go nowhere
tasks.brainstorm: Being actively thought about, no commitment yet
tasks.to_consider: Trap has seen it, not yet decided
tasks.agreed: Trap has confirmed this will be done — not yet started
tasks.in_progress: Actively being worked on this session or the last
tasks.done: Completed — add commit hash to notes
tasks.no_longer_relevant: Context has changed — was valid, now moot (LLM or trap judgement)
tasks.irrelevant: Was never really relevant — misclassified or superseded before starting

issues.resolved: Fixed — fill fix_summary and prevention fields

proposals.not_chosen: Trap did not select this option
proposals.pending: Offered but trap has not yet indicated a choice

severity_vocabulary.critical: Breaks functionality, introduces a security risk, or causes data loss — flag proactively even if trap did not ask
severity_vocabulary.warning: Degrades quality, violates a ruleset, creates tech debt, or is a poor implementation — log when discussed
severity_vocabulary.note: Cosmetic, style, dead code, minor redundancy — log when discussed

tasks.title: Create archive/ directory + repurpose status/index.html as hub
tasks.description: Create archive/index.html roster page for retired artifacts. Move Uplink Sentinel from status/index.html to archive/uplink-sentinel/index.html as a static preserved artifact (no probing, unknown status for dynamic nodes). Replace status/index.html with a minimal extensible hub page listing sub-sections under status/.
tasks.notes: Committed as pending. archive/uplink-sentinel/ is a static snapshot — no polling, no countdown. status/index.html is now a plain hub with no JS, extensible as new sub-pages are added.

tasks.title: Resolve all violations in structure-ruleset.md §12
tasks.description: 10 known structural violations documented in status/logs/structure-ruleset.md §12. Deletions first (llmtemp.json, llm-gen.json, plan-css-refactor.md), moves second (formatter/formatter.go → services/, tdo.txt → .ai/tdo.md, void.json → void/assets/data/), restructure last (dndm/ + radar/ + underdark/ → playground/ — requires explicit trap confirmation due to URL changes).
tasks.notes: Do not tackle during unrelated sessions. App directory moves require explicit trap confirmation before proceeding.

tasks.title: Build signal.json + Signal tab in logs viewer
tasks.description: Create signal.json as the mutable companion to logs.json. Four buckets: tasks, issues, proposals, migrated pending_work. Add Signal tab to status/logs/index.html with distinct visual treatment. Update logs.json to reference signal.json in _protocol and remove pending_work.
tasks.notes: Completed this session. signal.json created, Signal tab added with teal visual distinction, logs.json updated with companion_file reference and deprecated pending_work.

issues.title: llm.json v9.1.0: _index array removed, _index_matrix replaces it
issues.description: llm.json schema rewritten — _index flat array removed entirely. Three call sites still iterated _index to populate model <select> dropdowns, causing a silent crash (TypeError: undefined is not iterable). pay_per_token boolean also removed in favour of cost_tier string.
issues.reproduction_note: Load any page that renders a model <select> (playground, underdark) — dropdown is empty and throws TypeError in console because _index is undefined on the new schema.
issues.fix_summary: All three call sites updated to iterate _index_matrix.text (modality → provider → [ids]) grouped by provider. pay_per_token glyph logic replaced with cost_tier comparison ('Basically Free'/'Cheap' → ✦, else ◆). ui.js sub-counter updated to count cost_tier='Basically Free'.
issues.prevention: Before consuming llm.json in JS, check _meta.schema_version and verify expected top-level keys exist. When llm.json schema changes, grep for all _index and pay_per_token references immediately.
